tasks.title: Add reviewBeforePush and reviewBlockThreshold to PipelineConfig
tasks.description:
Extend the `PipelineConfig` type and its parser in `src/config/` to support two new fields:
- `reviewBeforePush: boolean` (default: `false`)
- `reviewBlockThreshold: 'critical' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'` (default: `'high'`)

Steps:
1. Locate the `PipelineConfig` interface (likely in `src/config/types.ts` or similar) and add the two fields with their types.
2. Update the config parser/validator to read these fields from `.telesis/config.yml`, applying defaults when absent.
3. Add unit tests covering: both fields present, neither present (defaults apply), invalid threshold value produces a typed error.
4. Do not touch the pipeline orchestrator yet — config changes only.
validationErrors: Update config parser to read reviewBeforePush from config.yml with default false when absent
Update config parser to read reviewBlockThreshold from config.yml with default 'high' when absent
Unit test: neither field present results in defaults being applied

tasks.title: Add --no-review CLI flag to telesis run command
tasks.description:
Update the `telesis run` command definition in `src/cli/run.ts` (or wherever the run command is defined under `src/cli/`) to accept a `--no-review` flag.

1. Add `.option('--no-review', 'Skip the review stage even if reviewBeforePush is enabled in config')` to the Commander command.
2. Pass the resolved flag value (boolean `skipReview`) through to whatever options object is forwarded to the pipeline orchestrator. If a `RunOptions` or similar type exists in `src/pipeline/`, add `skipReview?: boolean` to it.
3. Ensure the flag composes correctly with config: if `reviewBeforePush` is false in config OR `--no-review` is passed, the review stage is skipped.
4. No orchestrator logic yet — wiring only.

tasks.title: Add review stage events to the daemon event system
tasks.description:
Register two new pipeline lifecycle events so the rest of the system can observe the review stage outcome.

1. Find where pipeline events are defined (likely `src/pipeline/events.ts` or a shared events/types file). Add:
   - `'pipeline:review_passed'` — emitted when review runs and finds no blocking issues.
   - `'pipeline:review_failed'` — emitted when review runs and blocking findings are present.
2. Both events should carry a payload that includes at minimum: `workItemId: string`, `findingCount: number`, `blockingCount: number`, `threshold: string`.
3. Update any event type union or discriminated union so TypeScript is aware of the new events.
4. Update the TUI formatter / event display logic (wherever existing pipeline events like `pipeline:committed` or `pipeline:pushed` are formatted for output) to render human-readable messages for both new events.
5. Add unit tests for the formatter cases.

tasks.title: Extend RunResult to include review findings summary
Implement the review stage in the pipeline orchestrator
tasks.description:
Add the `reviewing` stage to the pipeline orchestrator in `src/pipeline/` between the `committing` and `pushing` stages.

1. After a successful commit, check whether the review stage should run:
   - `config.pipeline.reviewBeforePush === true` AND `options.skipReview !== true`
   - If not, proceed directly to pushing as before.
2. When the stage runs:
   a. Set pipeline stage to `'reviewing'`.
   b. Call the review business logic (from `src/review/` — do not shell out, call the function directly) against the committed diff/branch.
   c. Filter findings by the configured `reviewBlockThreshold` — a finding is blocking if its severity is >= the threshold (order: critical > high > medium > low).
   d. Populate `RunResult.reviewSummary` with the results.
   e. If blocking findings exist:
      - Emit `pipeline:review_failed` event with finding counts.
      - Set pipeline stage to `'review_failed'`.
      - Return the `RunResult` without pushing (do not throw — return a typed failure result).
   f. If no blocking findings:
      - Emit `pipeline:review_passed` event.
      - Proceed to the pushing stage.
3. Handle errors from the review call gracefully: if review itself throws unexpectedly, treat it as a non-blocking warning (log to stderr, emit `pipeline:review_passed` with a note) so a review tooling failure doesn't permanently block pushes — unless `reviewBeforePush` is strict-mode (not required for this issue, just handle the error path cleanly).
4. Unit tests must cover:
   - Review disabled (skipped correctly)
   - Review passes (push proceeds, event emitted)
   - Review fails with blocking findings (push blocked, stage set to `review_failed`)
   - Review skipped via `--no-review` flag
   - Severity threshold filtering (e.g. threshold=high blocks high+critical, not medium/low)
